title: IEnumerable<T> vs IQueryable<T>: делегаты и деревья выражений

cards.prompt: IEnumerable Where executes the delegate locally (LINQ to Objects), then Sum
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq; using System.Collections.Generic; IEnumerable<int> src = new List<int>{1,2,3,4}; var q = src.Where(x => x > 2); Console.WriteLine(q.Sum());

cards.prompt: The Where predicate over IEnumerable IS a Func<int,bool> delegate invoked per element
cards.code: using System; using System.Collections.Generic; Func<int,bool> pred = x => x > 2; var src = new List<int>{1,2,3,4}; int hits = 0; foreach (var x in src) if (pred(x)) hits++; Console.WriteLine($"hits={hits}");

cards.prompt: IEnumerable delegate is in-process C# code: the closure sees the mutated local at enumeration
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq; using System.Collections.Generic; int threshold = 2; IEnumerable<int> src = Enumerable.Range(1, 9); var q = src.Where(x => x > threshold); threshold = 6; Console.WriteLine($"count={q.Count()}");
